history.content:
<thought>
I can see the goal to my front-right, but directly in front of me is a lava cell (L), so I cannot move forward. To head toward the goal while avoiding lava, I should turn right first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can now see the goal (G) directly in front of me. The cell in front is G, not lava or wall, so I should move forward into it.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal (G) is no longer in front; I currently face a wall/blocked area ahead (there is a # directly in front). I need a clear path around, so I will turn left to search for an open floor toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
From my new facing, the cell directly in front is floor (.) rather than lava (#) or goal obstruction, and turning left places me to progress around the wall while keeping the goal visible to the right. I should move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am close to the lava region: the cell in front is a lava cell 'L', so moving forward would be deadly. I should turn right to avoid lava and seek the goal 'G' which is visible to my left/right area.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is immediately to my left. The cell in front is a floor '.', so I can move forward to approach the open corridor while keeping clear of any lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
